questions.question: What is the primary characteristic that distinguishes an Exclusive OR (XOR) gate from an OR gate in digital logic?
options: An XOR gate produces a HIGH output only when both inputs are HIGH, while an OR gate produces HIGH when either is HIGH.
An XOR gate produces a LOW output when both inputs are the same (both HIGH or both LOW), while an OR gate produces a HIGH output if any input is HIGH.
An XOR gate can be used as a universal gate, unlike an OR gate.
An XOR gate has only one input, whereas an OR gate has two or more.
An XOR gate provides buffered output for improved signal integrity, which an OR gate lacks.

questions.question: In a cache coherence protocol such as MESI (Modified, Exclusive, Shared, Invalid), what does the 'Invalid' state of a cache line primarily indicate?
options: The cache line contains the most up-to-date data, and this cache is the only one holding it.
The cache line is currently being modified by the local processor.
The data in the cache line is stale and must not be used, requiring a fresh copy from main memory or another cache.
The cache line is shared by multiple processors, all having read-only copies.
The cache line is locked and cannot be accessed by other processors.

questions.question: In the context of digital IC verification, what is the primary strength of Model Checking as a formal verification technique, distinct from simulation-based verification?
options: It executes the design much faster than traditional simulation, reducing total verification time.
It can exhaustively explore all possible states and input sequences of a finite-state system to mathematically prove the absence of design errors.
It requires less computational resources compared to running extensive test benches.
It automatically generates test vectors for specific corner cases that are hard to reach via random simulation.
It provides real-time performance metrics by executing the design on a physical prototype.

questions.question: In deep sub-micron digital ICs, which reliability concern arises from the momentum transfer between flowing electrons and metal ions in interconnects, causing material displacement and potentially leading to opens or shorts over time?
options: Negative Bias Temperature Instability (NBTI)
Hot Carrier Injection (HCI)
Electromigration
Dielectric Breakdown
Thermal Runaway

questions.question: Within a Central Processing Unit (CPU), what is the primary function of the Control Unit?
options: To perform all arithmetic and logical operations on data.
To store program instructions and data for quick access.
To generate timing and control signals that direct the flow of data and execution of instructions.
To manage the virtual memory mapping and page tables.
To temporarily hold data being processed by the ALU.
